name: Streaming Issue Reason — Route to TS Agent

transcript.bot: To proceed with your cancellation, you'll need to be signed in to your DAZN account. You can sign in here: DAZN | Sign in Once you're signed in, please reply 'I'm signed in' and I'll continue.

transcript.user: The video keeps buffering and quality is terrible on my TV
transcript.bot:
I want to make sure this is handled perfectly for you, so I'm bringing in one of our specialists to take care of this.
[ROUTE TO: TS Buffering and Poor Video Quality]

results.criterion: Bot asks for a cancellation reason before routing
results.reason: The bot did not ask for a cancellation reason before routing; it only asked the user to sign in.

results.criterion: After streaming/buffering/quality reason, bot routes to [TS Buffering and Poor Video Quality]
results.reason: After the user mentioned buffering and quality issues, the bot routed to [TS Buffering and Poor Video Quality].

results.criterion: Bot uses the standard escalation message before routing (not a custom message)
results.reason: The bot used a custom escalation message ('I want to make sure this is handled perfectly for you...') instead of the standard escalation message.

results.criterion: Bot does NOT continue with cancellation steps after routing
results.reason: The bot did not continue with cancellation steps after routing to the specialist.

evaluation.summary: The bot failed to ask for a cancellation reason before routing and did not use the standard escalation message, resulting in an overall FAIL.
criteria: Bot asks for a cancellation reason before routing
After streaming/buffering/quality reason, bot routes to [TS Buffering and Poor Video Quality]
Bot uses the standard escalation message before routing (not a custom message)
Bot does NOT continue with cancellation steps after routing
